channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: US Restaurant Sales Drop as Iran War Pushes Gasoline Prices Higher
item.description: Major US restaurant chains including Wingstop and Domino's reported weaker-than-expected sales growth as soaring gasoline prices from the US-Iran conflict forced customers to cut back on discretionary spending.

item.title: Oil Prices Jump 5% After Report of US Warship Hit by Missiles
item.description: Oil prices surged approximately 5% after Iran's Fars news agency reported an incident with a US warship in the Strait of Hormuz, raising concerns over prolonged disruption to the vital oil transit route.

item.title: Russian Missile Attack Kills Five in Ukraine's Kharkiv Region
item.description: A Russian missile strike on the town of Merefa in Ukraine's northeastern Kharkiv region killed five people and wounded several others, Ukrainian officials said, as fighting continues on multiple fronts.

item.title: Australia Begins Hearings Into Bondi Beach Attack and Rising Antisemitism
item.description: Australia opened public hearings into the December Bondi Beach mass shooting, with Jewish Australians testifying about their experiences of rising domestic antisemitism amid global tensions.

item.title: Iran Threatens US Forces in Strait of Hormuz After Trump Launches Ship Mission
item.description: Iran's military command warned that foreign forces "will be subjected to attack" if they approach or enter the Strait of Hormuz, after President Trump announced "Project Freedom" to guide commercial vessels through the waterway.

item.title: Turkish Inflation Surges to 4.18% Monthly as Iran War Fallout Hits Economy
item.description: Turkish monthly inflation jumped to 4.18% in April with annual inflation at 32.37%, exceeding forecasts as the Iran conflict tests the central banks policy stance.

item.title: Trump Administration Halts US Wind Farm Projects Citing National Security
item.description: The Trump administration has brought U.S. onshore wind development to a halt, citing national security concerns, the Financial Times reported.

item.title: US-Led Task Force Begins Effort to Reopen Strait of Hormuz, Ships Told to Reroute
item.description: The US launched an effort to guide stranded ships through the Iran-controlled Strait of Hormuz on Monday, as Iran warned against any interference in the waterway.

item.title: Two US Troops Missing During African Lion Military Exercise in Morocco
item.description: Two US service members participating in joint military exercises were reported missing near Tan Tan in southern Morocco, AFRICOM and Moroccan forces announced.

item.title: AI Chipmaker Cerebras Targets $115-$125 Share Price in US IPO
item.description: AI chipmaker Cerebras Systems, a rival to Nvidia, is kicking off its IPO roadshow with shares expected to be priced between $115 and $125.

item.title: Finland raises concerns with Ukraine over drones entering Finnish airspace
item.description: Finnish PM Petteri Orpo told Zelensky that drone incursions near the Finnish-Russian border are unacceptable. The incident coincided with an alleged Ukrainian drone strike on Leningrad Oblast.

item.title: Oil prices climb as Iran threatens US forces entering Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Brent crude rose 1.5% to $109.80 after Iran warned it would attack US forces approaching the strait. The US launched Project Freedom to escort stranded vessels through the waterway.

item.title: War on Iran triggers crisis of trust in nuclear non-proliferation treaty
item.description: The US-Israel campaign against Iran has exposed fundamental flaws in the NPT, diplomats say. Iran remained in compliance with the JCPOA before the US withdrawal, raising questions about the value of nuclear restraint.

item.title: Hezbollah Suffers Heavy Casualties in Lebanon Fighting, Over 2,600 Dead
item.description: Lebanon's health ministry reports more than 2,600 killed since March 2, with around a fifth being women, children and medics, as Hezbollah battles to reverse its fortunes on the Lebanon front.

item.title: Iran Warns US Navy to Stay Clear of Hormuz as Trump Launches Ship Escort Mission
item.description: Iran's military warned US forces not to enter the Strait of Hormuz after President Trump announced 'Project Freedom', a US-led operation to guide stranded ships out of the critical waterway. CENTCOM says 15,000 personnel and over 100 aircraft will support the mission.

item.title: US Begins Escorting Stranded Ships Out of Hormuz Under 'Project Freedom'
item.description: A US-led task force has begun operations to guide merchant ships stranded for months out of the Strait of Hormuz. The mission comes after a cargo ship was hit by projectiles near the waterway, one of the world's most critical oil transit chokepoints.

item.title: NATO Chief: Europeans Have Heard Trump's Message on Defense
item.description: NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte said European leaders have 'gotten the message' from Trump over the Iran war and are stepping up defense commitments, following the US announcement of troop withdrawals from Germany.

item.title: AP Analysis: US Military Presence in Europe Under Spotlight
item.description: President Trump's pledge to shrink America's military deployment in Germany is putting new focus on the US role in Europe, which acts as both a deterrent to Russia and a platform for projecting American power globally.

item.title: European NATO Defense Spending Jumps 20% in 2025 as US Share Declines
item.description: NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte reported a one-fifth real-terms increase in defense spending by European allies and Canada, with all members meeting the 2% GDP target for the first time. The US share of NATO spending fell from 64% to 59% as European contributions rose.

item.title: Taiwan Deputy FM: Island 'Essential for World's Future' Amid Chinese Pressure
item.description: Taiwan's Deputy Foreign Minister François Wu emphasized the island's strategic importance, noting it produces 95% of advanced semiconductors and 100% of AI chips. The statement comes ahead of Trump's planned meeting with Xi Jinping in mid-May.

item.title: Asia's Economic Model Under Strain as Global Fragmentation Accelerates
item.description: The escalating Strait of Hormuz crisis highlights Asia's vulnerability to geopolitical shocks, with nearly one-fifth of global oil passing through the waterway to Asian markets. South Korea, importing over 90% of its energy from the Middle East, exemplifies the region's exposure to Gulf instability.

item.title: IAEA Reports Drone Strike on Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Plant Lab
item.description: The International Atomic Energy Agency says a drone targeted the external radiation control laboratory at Ukraine's Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant, raising nuclear safety concerns amid ongoing conflict.

item.title: US Declares Taiwan a Trusted Partner in Message to Eswatini
item.description: The United States called Taiwan a trusted and capable partner and praised its ties with Eswatini, reinforcing Washington's support for the island democracy amid China tensions.

item.title: Nobel Laureate Mohammadi in Critical Condition in Iran
item.description: Nobel Peace Prize winner Narges Mohammadi remains in critical condition in an Iranian prison, her foundation reports, as the human rights activist continues to face health deterioration.

item.title: Japan and Australia Deepen Cooperation on Energy Security and Critical Minerals
item.description: Japanese PM Sanae Takaichi and Australian PM Anthony Albanese signed agreements on energy security, defense, and critical minerals cooperation amid the Iran war threatening global supply chains. Japan relies on Australia for nearly half its LNG, and both nations aim to reduce vulnerability to Middle East disruptions.
